strict_win_deltas.delta: P1 adds a coherent branch in the 'version' case that parses --format, validates it, and switches output between bare string and JSON.stringify({version}), while P2's diff is empty and implements no version/--format logic at all.

P1 explicitly checks `formatIndex !== -1 && format !== 'json'` and calls process.exit(1) with an error message for unsupported formats like yaml, whereas P2 has no code at all and therefore cannot exit 1 on bad input.

P1 satisfies every stated requirement (JSON single-line output, bare version preserved, exit 1 on bad format, tests added, only bin/cli.js and tests/cli.test.js touched) while P2's empty diff satisfies none of them.

P1 updates the USAGE string to document `version [--format json]` and adds two focused tests (`version prints JSON with --format json` and `version rejects unsupported formats`), giving future maintainers both documentation and regression coverage that P2 (no changes) provides none of.
